published_prior_art.cite: CarrascoFlorensRenault2007 plus standard finite KKT convex duality: metric-minimal exact-fiber selector formula

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Conjecture 1 already proposed a finite OR/DR-vs-minimax native-equivalence iff frontier with first-order object

published_prior_art.cite: none; KallusMaoUehara2021 and CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024 do not give an OR/DR-vs-minimax commutator frontier

repo_prior_art.cite: eid_proximal_phase_v1: Conjecture 2 already targets native selection-range separation after ATE-active bridge nonuniqueness

structure_flags.label: Bibliography
structure_flags.one_line: The ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 entry is unstable/inaccurate: the verified article is Statistics & Probability Letters 198 (2023), 109836, while 2024/110055 refers to a corrigendum record.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship comparator claims cite KallusMaoUehara2021 and CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024 only at paper level; no section/equation/page anchor ties the alleged selector-equivalence gap to either paper.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023 and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 comparisons are bare bibkey-level anchors, too thin for a flagship open-class separation claim.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist omits BennettKallusMaoNeweySyrgkanisUehara2023COLT/MinimaxIV, which are closer weak-nuisance and minimax-selector comparators for the first-order equivalence claim.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The proposed concrete witness uses T=(1 1), which cannot be a conditional distribution row; without a valid finite observed law, the generic open-class claim remains below novelty_target=flagship.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1 / Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The population metrics G^R and G^M are only said to be 'induced by' OR/DR and minimax selectors; the actual published population objectives are not defined, so the named selector comparison is not pinned.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Native-equivalence through first order is stated iff Gamma_nat=0 and Gamma_IF=0, but Gamma_nat is exactly the selected ATE discrepancy and Gamma_IF is the norm of its derivative.

soundness_flags.label: Example 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The witness matrix T=(1 1) violates the setup definition T_{z,w}=Pr(W=w|A,Z,X), whose rows must be probability vectors.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the tautological iff with an independently characterized observable object: explicit formulas for G^R and G^M from the published criteria plus a nontrivial algebraic condition not defined as the discrepancy itself.

Repair the witness by giving a full finite observed law or at least stochastic matrices, e.g. T=(1/2,1/2), m=1/2 with probabilities for A,Z,W,Y that realize the bridge equation and nonzero commutator.

Add location-level anchors to KallusMaoUehara2021, CuiPuShiMiaoTchetgen2024, ZhangLiMiaoTchetgen2023, and GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023, and add Bennett et al. 2023 as the weak-nuisance/minimax comparator chain.

Demote Theorem 1 to support algebra and make the flagship claim a strict extension beyond eid_proximal_phase_v1, explicitly explaining why the commutator is not just a fixed-metric specialization of tau_native.

recommended_next_step: Revise rather than pivot: pin the published selector metrics, fix the invalid witness, and restate Conjecture 1 as a non-tautological frontier capable of clearing the flagship floor.
